THE PARTIES MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ALL PAPERS FILED IN SUPPORT AND OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 18, 2013 AT 4:00 PM. NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR JUN-23-2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON DEC-18-2013 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH ALVARADO'S MOTION FOR SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILLING ANOTHER MOTION.. THE COURT UNDERSTANDS THE MOTION TO BE A REQUEST TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. THE MOTION IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1324. PLAINTIFF DID NOT ATTACH THE PROPOSED AMENDED PLEADING THAT HE SEEKS TO FILE. (CRC 3.1324(A).). PLAINTIFF DID NOT ATTACH A SEPARATE DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY. (CRC 3.1324(B).) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JOSEPH MCGOVERN AND LISA MCGOVERN'S MOTION FOR MANDATORY DISMISSAL FOR DELAY IN BRINGING ACTION TO TRIAL IS GRANTED. CAL. CODE CIV. PROC. 583.310 PROVIDES THAT "AN ACTION SHALL BE BROUGHT TO TRIAL WITHIN FIVE YEARS AFTER THE ACTION IS COMMENCED AGAINST THE DEFENDANT." THIS STATUTE IS MANDATORY. (WIGGINS V. WASHINGTON NAT. LIFE INS. CO. (1966) 246 CAL.APP.2D 840, 848.) THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED ON NOVEMBER 19, 2008. THEREFORE, THE FIVE-YEAR PERIOD EXPIRED ON NOVEMBER 19, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion For Mandatory Dismissal For Delay In Bringing Action To Trial
Off Calendar Dec-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ERIC WILLIAMS, ESQ., 510.267.7250, FOR DEFENDANTS JOSEPH AND LISA MCGOVERN; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JOSEPH MCGOVERN and LISA MCGOVERN'S Motion For Mandatory Dismissal For Delay In Bringing Action To Trial IS Granted. Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 583.310 provides that "an action shall be brought to trial within five years after the action is commenced against the defendant." This statute is mandatory. (Wiggins v. Washington Nat. Life Ins. Co. (1966) 246 Cal.App.2d 840, 848.) The complaint was filed on November 19, 2008. Therefore, the five-year period expired on November 19, 2013. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Supplemental Complaint;
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF JOSEPH ALVARADO'S Motion For Supplemental Complaint is denied without prejudice to refilling another motion.. The Court understands the motion to be a request to amend the complaint. The motion is denied because Plaintiff's motion does not comply with the requirements California Rule of Court 3.1324. Plaintiff did not attach the proposed amended pleading that he seeks to file. (CRC 3.1324(a).). Plaintiff did not attach a separate declaration under penalty of perjury. (CRC 3.1324(b).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Notice Of Motion For Mandatory Dismissal For Delay In Bringing Action To Trial
Off Calendar Dec-02-2013 Continued To Dec-20-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs JOSEPH MCGOVERN and LISA MCGOVERN's Motion For Mandatory Dismissal For Delay In Bringing Action To Trial is continued to December 20, 2013, to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 2, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH ALVARDO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Off calendar as untimely under CCP 437c(a). The motion for summary judgment was filed and served on October 23, 2013, which is less than 75 days notice. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
